lessons.title: APIRequestContext Does Not Support route() Interception — Use Method Override Instead
lessons.context: Playwright's page.route() intercepts network requests made by the browser page context. APIRequestContext makes direct HTTP calls that bypass the browser entirely — so page.route() has no effect on them.

problem.error: page.route() mock has no effect on APIRequestContext calls
problem.cause: page.route() only intercepts browser-initiated requests — APIRequestContext bypasses the browser network stack

antipattern.description: Using page.route() to mock responses for APIRequestContext
antipattern.why_bad: APIRequestContext makes direct HTTP calls — page.route() never fires, mock is silently ignored
code: // BAD — route() has no effect on APIRequestContext
// This mock is never triggered when using apiContext.get('/api/users')

solution.description: Override the service method directly in the Arrange step to inject mock behavior.
code: // GOOD — override service method in Arrange step
const mockResponse = { users: [{ id: 1, name: 'Mock User' }] };

lessons.ai_instruction: When mocking API responses in API-layer tests using APIRequestContext, use method override in the Arrange step instead of page.route(). Never use page.route() for APIRequestContext mocking.

lessons.title: Module-Level Store in DatabaseHelper Causes Parallel Test Data Leakage
lessons.context: When tests run in parallel, shared module-level state causes data from one test to bleed into another. A module-level const store = {} is initialized once per module load and shared across all parallel test workers.

problem.error: Tests interfere with each other when running in parallel
problem.cause: Module-level store is shared across all parallel test workers

antipattern.description: Using module-level variables to store test state
antipattern.why_bad: Module-level state is shared across parallel workers — test A's data appears in test B's assertions
code: // BAD — module-level store shared across parallel tests
const store: Record<string, User> = {}; // Shared by all workers!

solution.description: Move store into class instance (private maps) so each test instantiation has isolated state.
code: // GOOD — instance-level store, isolated per test

lessons.ai_instruction: When generating DatabaseHelper classes, always use private instance Maps instead of module-level variables. This ensures parallel test isolation.

lessons.title: In-Memory Mock DB Pattern for Tests Without Real Database
lessons.context: When no real database is available (demo, CI without DB, POC), an in-memory Map-based DatabaseHelper can fully replace real DB operations. The key is using a testId prefix on all seeded data to prevent collision between parallel tests.

problem.error: Tests cannot run without a real database connection
problem.cause: Test infrastructure requires real database that isn't available in all environments

antipattern.description: Hard dependency on real database for all environments
antipattern.why_bad: Blocks testing in demo, CI-lite, or POC environments
code: // BAD — hard dependency on real database

solution.description: Use in-memory Map as a drop-in replacement. Use testId prefix to prevent data collision between parallel tests.
code: async seedPatient(testId: string, data: PatientData): Promise<string> {

lessons.ai_instruction: When generating DatabaseHelper for environments without real DB, use in-memory Map with testId-prefixed keys. Always implement seed(), get(), and cleanup(testId) methods.
